column1: FOREIGN SUPPLIER VERIFICATION PROGRAM (FSVP)  ·  21 CFR Part 1 Subpart L
Mandatory for ALL imported food ingredients under FSMA  ·  Annual re-verification required

column2: Identify all known or reasonably foreseeable hazards (biological, chemical, physical, radiological) — include GMO/BE-derived allergen cross-contamination risk for BE-listed crop derivatives
Evaluate foreign supplier's food safety processes before first import and annually — include GMO status documentation and NBFDS compliance verification
column4: Supplier evaluation report; CoA review; GMO declaration on file

column2: At minimum: CoA review or on-site audit. High-risk hazards (Salmonella, heavy metals, BE allergen cross-contact): annual on-site audit required
column4: Audit report OR CoA batch records + PCR GMO test if applicable

column2: Documented response when supplier fails verification — including BE/GMO disclosure failures or IP chain-of-custody breaks
All FSVP activities documented; available to FDA within 24 hours of request; retained 2–3 years; GMO declarations and PCR test records are FSVP records
column4: FSVP master file per supplier + GMO/IP documentation

column2: FSVP importer (US agent) must be identified for every import entry
column4: FDA Prior Notice; Import entry documents

column2: Re-evaluate if: new hazard, supplier process change, FDA alert, material non-conformance, OR change in GMO status of ingredient (e.g. new approved BE event in supply)
column4: Updated evaluation report; GMO status change notification

column2: Check FDA Import Alert database quarterly. Suppliers on import alert = FSVP automatic fail. Note: USDA AMS monitors NBFDS compliance separately.
column4: FDA Import Alert screenshot; USDA AMS compliance file

column2: All FSVP activities reviewed by FSVP Qualified Individual (QI) — must also understand NBFDS GMO disclosure requirements for BE-listed ingredients
column4: QI credentials; training records incl. NBFDS training
